chapter_context.summary: Contains the famous descriptions of the Rose of Sharon and the Lily of the Valleys, the groom's invitation to arise and come away in the spring, and warnings against the little foxes.

outline.text: The Rose of Sharon; The Banquet House and the Charge to the Daughters
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Rose of Sharon; The Banquet House and the Charge to the Daughters (2:1-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Song Of Solomon.

outline.text: The Beloved's Voice and His Invitation to Arise and Come Away in Springtime
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Beloved's Voice and His Invitation to Arise and Come Away in Springtime (2:8-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Song Of Solomon.

outline.text: The Little Foxes; The Bride's Confidence in Her Beloved
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Little Foxes; The Bride's Confidence in Her Beloved (2:14-17), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Song Of Solomon.

key_themes.title: The Beauty of Marital Love
key_themes.description: Song of Solomon celebrates the physical, emotional, and spiritual dimensions of romantic love, presenting it as a beautiful creation of God.

key_themes.title: Unbreakable Union
key_themes.description: The poem highlights the intense desire, commitment, and unbreakable bond between the lovers, reflecting the covenant love between God and His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the passionate expressions of Chapter 2, how does this celebration of romantic love prompt you to give thanks for the emotional and relational gifts God has designed?
How can the commitment and intense desire described in this chapter inspire your own covenant relationship with God?

cultural_historical.title: Hebrew Wedding Poetry (Wasfs)
cultural_historical.text: The book contains multiple examples of 'wasfs'—traditional Near Eastern wedding songs that praise the physical features and virtues of the bride or groom in rich, agricultural metaphors.
